scenario: Helios Healthcare schedules 4200 tasks per week in Camunda 8.8 using FEEL expressions on a routing gateway. A condition needs to flag shifts whose start date falls on a Monday by reading the day-of-week number from the shift start date so the planner can rebalance the 7-day roster.
question: Which FEEL property on a date value returns the day of the week as a number where 1 is Monday?

options.text: The weekday property on a date or date-time value returns a number in the range 1..7 where 1 represents Monday in FEEL
The day property on a date or date-time value returns a number in the range 1..7 where 1 represents Monday in FEEL
The month property on a date or date-time value returns a number in the range 1..7 where 1 represents Monday in FEEL
The dayOfWeek property on a date or date-time value returns a number in the range 1..7 where 1 represents Monday in FEEL

a.text: Correct. The FEEL temporal properties table documents weekday as the property on date and date-time values returning a number in 1..7 where 1 is Monday.

b.text: Incorrect. The day property returns the day of the month in the range 1..31, not the day of the week. It is not the property documented for weekday lookups.

c.text: Incorrect. The month property returns the month of the year as a number in 1..12 where 1 is January, not a day-of-week value in 1..7.

d.text: Incorrect. FEEL does not expose a dayOfWeek property on temporal values; the documented property name is weekday, not a camelCase alias.

explanation: Date and date-time values in FEEL expose a weekday property that returns the day of the week as a number in 1..7 where 1 represents Monday. Other properties such as day, month, year, and hour cover different components of the temporal value.
